title: SJR 14 calls on Congress to close the tax loophole that allowed LA Dodgers player Shohei Ohtani to avoid paying approximately $90 million in state taxes.
prompt_preview:
[1 chunks] Task: Extract all distinct claims about AI or emerging technology in this press release. Do NOT assume a fixed number.
Output JSON only matching the provided schema.
Return concise, standalone claim_texts that could be tested against scientific literature. Exclude purely procedural statem

raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "The Los Angeles Dodgers signed Shohei Ohtani to a ten-year, $700 million contract.",
    "Ohtani's contract allows him to defer $68 million annually, receiving only $2 million per year for ten years.",
    "Deferring compensation may enable Ohtani to avoid paying over $90 million in state taxes if he resides outside California when the deferred compensation is paid.",
    "Current federal and state laws permit unlimited deferrals of compensation for high-income earners.",
    "The absence of a cap on deferred compensation for high-income earners contributes to an unbalanced tax structure and exacerbates income inequality.",
    "Potential limits on deferred compensation for high-income earners could contribute to a more equitable tax system.",
    "Establishing a reasonable cap on deferred compensation is urged to rectify tax imbalances and promote equity."
  ]
}
```
